slides.title: Beat the *post-lunch crash* with a walk.
slides.sub: Ten minutes of walking after you eat can flatten the energy dip that wrecks your afternoon.

slides.title: Why lunch makes you tired
slides.body: A big meal sends blood sugar up, then down. That crash *feels like exhaustion* by 2pm.

slides.title: Walk for ten minutes
slides.body: A *10-minute walk* after eating helps your muscles soak up glucose — so the spike is gentler.

slides.title: Muscles use the sugar
slides.body: Moving muscles pull glucose from your blood without extra insulin, which *can support* steadier energy.

slides.title: No gym required
slides.body: This isn't a workout. A slow loop around the block or the office is *enough to count*.

slides.title: Attach it to lunch
slides.body: Tie the walk to a meal you already eat. *Same time, every day* makes it automatic.

slides.title: Turn lunch into *steady energy*.
slides.body: Vibe Health counts those post-meal steps toward your daily score, so the small walks actually add up.

caption:
That 2pm wall after lunch? You can walk right through it. 🚶

Ten easy minutes after you eat smooths out the blood-sugar spike that makes you crash. No gym, no sweat. Save this one. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those post-meal steps into points on your daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: The 10-Minute Walk That Kills the Afternoon Crash
